standard: IEEE 1016 SDD -> Embedded C Implementation Guide

global_coding_rules.rule: MISRA-C 2012 compliance required.
No dynamic memory allocation - malloc, calloc, realloc, free are forbidden.
No recursion of any kind.
Single entry point, single exit point per function.
Maximum 50 lines of executable code per function.
All functions must have a Doxygen comment: @brief, @param, @return, @trace.
All global variables must be static in main.c, extern-declared in main.h.
Use fixed-width types only: uint8_t, uint16_t, uint32_t, int8_t, int16_t, int32_t, bool.
All #define macros must be UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.
All function names must be lower_snake_case.
Struct and typedef names must be PascalCase with _t suffix (e.g. SensorData_t).
Error codes must use a typedef enum, not magic numbers.
Hardware register access must use volatile pointers or CMSIS/HAL macros.
ISR functions must be prefixed ISR_ and must only set a flag then return.
No floating-point unless hardware FPU is confirmed available.
All arrays must be fixed-size; size defined by a #define constant.
All pointer parameters must be checked for NULL before dereferencing.
No implicit type conversions - cast explicitly.
Code files are main.c and main.h ONLY - no new files.
Every function declaration in main.h, definition in main.c.

hard_rule:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINT - CODE CHANGES ONLY IN main.c AND main.h:
  - All new functions, declarations, includes, macros, typedefs, and global
    variables derived from this design MUST be placed exclusively in main.c
    or main.h.
  - Do NOT create any new .c or .h files.
  - Do NOT modify any existing file other than main.c or main.h.
  - Do NOT move or refactor code that already exists in other files.
  - If a component logically belongs in a separate module, implement it
    as a static function inside main.c and declare it in main.h.
  - This rule overrides all other code organisation preferences.
